target.title: feat(gateway): wire read coding tool into HTTP /tools/invoke (narrow)
target.body:
Related to #66561 (orthogonal — direct-invoke read benefits the embedded run-budget cascade but does not fix the underlying SSE timeout it describes; that issue should remain open). narrow first landing of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Wire the `read` coding tool into the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver) so it is reachable for deterministic automation — CI/preflight checks, lint pipelines, browser capture flows — without an LLM round-trip.

**This PR is the redesigned narrow read-only landing.** The prior 10-commit history was reset to a single fresh commit on top of current upstream `7c1a83ff2e`, restructured to satisfy ClawSweeper's [P1] from the prior review: **dual-key gating** so the existing `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` config knob does not silently grant host-FS read on upgrade.

## Dual-key gating (addresses ClawSweeper [P1])

To enable `read` on the direct-invoke surface, operators must set BOTH:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` | **NEW.** Distinct surface-specific opt-in. Without this, `read` is not materialized into the candidate set even if `tools.allow` includes it. | `false` (omitted) |
| `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` | Lifts `read` from `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (now extended with `"read"`). Existing knob; keeps operator-acceptance step visible in the standard policy surface. | not present |

**Either key alone leaves `read` unreachable.** Old configs that already contain `allow: ["read"]` for unrelated reasons (e.g. an MCP/agent surface where `read` was always available) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer — no silent upgrade-time expansion.

| `tools.allow` includes `"read"` | `directInvoke.hostFsRead` | `read` reachable on direct-invoke |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | ❌ |
| yes | no   | ❌ (tool not materialized) |
| no  | yes  | ❌ (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Read-only.** Mutating coding primitives (`write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` / `exec` / `process`) are NOT exposed by this PR.
- **Both surfaces.** Gating applies to HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND the SDK-facing JSON-RPC `tools.invoke` (they share `resolveGatewayScopedTools`).
- **MCP loopback unaffected.** `surface === "loopback"` is not gated.
- **Hook-bypass safe.** Uses new `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export (module-private Symbol marker, not reachable from typed SDK params) so HTTP-surface tools aren't double-wrapped — `handleToolsInvokeHttp` already runs `runBeforeToolCallHook` itself before dispatch.

## Files changed

- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — `SKIP_BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_HOOK` symbol + `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export
- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — `GatewayToolsConfig.directInvoke: GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` field (new type)
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — `"read"` added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — dual-key gated materialization of `read` into candidate set
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` finding (only fires when BOTH gates set; silent for either alone since `read` is unreachable there)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — 5 new test cases covering all 4 gate combinations + vanilla
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` — operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs with config example + truth table

## Code-level test coverage

`src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` (new `describe` block):

- ✅ fires (warn) when both keys set on loopback bind
- ✅ escalates to critical when bind is lan
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `allow: ["read"]` (legacy shape)
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `hostFsRead: true`
- ✅ does NOT fire on vanilla config

## Why a single fresh commit (not rebase of prior 10)

The original PR's 10 commits implemented an OLD design (single-key `allow: ["read"]` as the opt-in). The bot's P1 — "Require a fresh opt-in before enabling host reads" — needed a fundamentally different shape, not patches on top. Plus the prior commits referenced `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` which has been renamed to `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` upstream. Cleaner to reset to upstream/main and write the redesign as one commit than rebase-then-patch.

Backup of prior PR history retained at `backup/85664-pre-rebase-20260603-onto-7c1a83ff2e` on the public fork.

---

## Real behavior proof

> **Note (head `b264b15a7a`):** The live proof below ran at `0a0cafdef7`. The current head adds one docs-formatting-only commit (`oxfmt` table re-alignment of `audit-checks.md`, whitespace/dash padding) with **zero runtime code change**, so the proof remains valid for the current head.

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Expose the `read` coding tool over Gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver) for deterministic automation, gated behind a NEW distinct `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` opt-in. Addresses ClawSweeper [P1]: a single-key `allow: ["read"]` legacy config must NOT silently grant host-FS read on upgrade.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `git checkout 0a0cafdef7 && docker run --name oc-85664-proof -v $PWD:/work -v oc_proof_nm:/work/node_modules --platform linux/arm64 -e NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=12288 node:22-bookworm-slim bash /work/.build-and-proof-85664.sh` — exit 0.

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from head `0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf` inside `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (pnpm v11.2.2, linux-arm64 packages in named volume). Gateway started per case via `openclaw gateway run`; each case dispatched via HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK `GatewayClientTransport` WebSocket `oc.tools.invoke(...)`.
Unit tests (Docker, linux/arm64): 115 total — 10/10 (audit-gateway-tools-http) + 105/105 (tools-invoke-http). Full build + SDK build: all phases completed.

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, head `0a0cafdef7`):** from `docker run node:22-bookworm-slim` (linux/arm64):

```
=== HEAD: 0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf ===

### TESTS (audit-gateway-tools-http) ###
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       10 passed (10)

### TESTS (tools-invoke-http) ###
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       105 passed (105)

### BUILD ###
[build-all] plugins:assets:build done in 407ms
[build-all] tsdown done in 242.8s
BUILD OK

### SDK BUILD ###
dist/index.mjs  30.52 kB
SDK BUILD OK

### LIVE PROOF ###

--- R-DENIED-no-opt-in ---
  [cfg] allow=["read"] hfsR=false
  [gw] ready
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}
  [SDK] ok=false {"ok":false,"toolName":"read","error":{"code":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}

--- R-ENABLED ---
  [cfg] allow=["read"] hfsR=true
  [gw] ready
  [HTTP] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF READ SOURCE at 0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf\n"}]}}
  [SDK] ok=true {"ok":true,"toolName":"read","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF READ SOURCE at 0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf\n"}]},"source":"core"}

--- R-DENIED-no-allow ---
  [cfg] allow=[] hfsR=true
  [gw] ready
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}
  [SDK] ok=false {"ok":false,"toolName":"read","error":{"code":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}

--- WRITE-still-denied ---
  [cfg] allow=["write"] hfsR=true
  [gw] ready
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  [SDK] ok=false {"ok":false,"toolName":"write","error":{"code":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

=== FINAL FILE STATES ===
[read-source]: PROOF READ SOURCE at 0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=0a0cafdef7d0a61ae87e6836272beb7f199aefdf ===
```

**Observed result after fix (head `0a0cafdef7`):** All 8 dispatches (4 cases × HTTP+SDK) from `docker run node:22-bookworm-slim` behave identically. R-DENIED-no-opt-in: `allow=["read"]` alone → 404 on both surfaces (P1 fix: legacy config stays inert). R-ENABLED: both keys set → HTTP 200 with actual sentinel file content, SDK ok=true with same content. R-DENIED-no-allow: `hostFsRead=true` alone → 404 (proves both keys required). WRITE-still-denied: `allow=["write"]` with `hostFsRead=true` → 404 for `write` (read-only scope confirmed).

**What was not tested:** Write/edit/exec/process are out of scope for this read-only PR (covered by #63919 for write/edit). `apply_patch` is in deny list, not yet factory-produced.

## Docs discoverability

Addresses ClawSweeper's [P2]: "Document the host-FS read opt-in everywhere" (`src/config/zod-schema.ts:1037-1040`).

- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — FIELD_HELP entries for `gateway.tools.directInvoke` and `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead`.
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` — paragraph describing the dual-key gating + audit warning hookup.
- `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — catalog row for `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` with severity, criteria, and config-key references.

So operators using config help/lookup or security-audit docs can now discover this security-sensitive opt-in and warning.

target.title: fix(telegram): avoid silent truncation in partial streaming finals
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Telegram partial-stream finals could be marked as delivered before continuation chunks were actually sent, which could suppress visible fallback handling and leave users with a mid-sentence cutoff.
- Solution: Delay Telegram delivered-state marking until continuation chunks succeed, treat suppressed follow-up sends as delivery failures, and repair preview-finalized messages from the transcript-backed final text when needed.
- What changed: Added Telegram delivery-state fixes and regression tests for thrown and false-return follow-up failures, plus preview-finalized transcript-backed repair coverage.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change diagnostic stuck-session recovery behavior; that work was split into follow-up PR #84666.

## Motivation

Explain why this change should exist now. Link it to the user pain, failure mode, maintainer need, or product goal. If this is purely mechanical, write `N/A`.

- Telegram delivery could fail in a way that looked successful to the gateway, producing silent truncation for real users.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #49889
- Related #84666
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only. Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes; terminal screenshots and copied live output count. Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram partial-stream final continuation failure should no longer be treated as successfully delivered, and preview-finalized Telegram text should be repairable from transcript-backed final text.
- Real environment tested: Pending maintainer or contributor run against a real Telegram bot/chat setup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Pending real-environment proof run.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Pending.
- Observed result after fix: Pending.
- What was not tested: Real Telegram after-fix behavior has not yet been captured in this contributor environment.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Source-level repro and regression tests cover the failure mode; no attached real Telegram before/after capture yet.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: Telegram streamed-final follow-up chunks were sent after delivery state could already be marked successful, so a later follow-up failure could leave the system believing the message had been delivered even though the user saw a truncated final.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Follow-up sends that returned false were not treated as delivery failure, and preview-finalized message content was not repaired from the transcript-backed canonical final text.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts` and `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Follow-up chunk sends that throw or return false must prevent delivered-state success for streamed finals, and preview-finalized messages should repair from transcript-backed final text.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These unit tests exercise the exact delivery-state and repair decisions at the seam where the regression occurred without requiring a live Telegram environment.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing Telegram delivery tests covered adjacent streaming behavior but not the suppressed follow-up failure path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Telegram users should now get visible failure handling instead of silent mid-sentence truncation when streamed final follow-up delivery fails.
- Telegram preview-finalized replies can be repaired to the transcript-backed final text when the finalized preview content is truncated.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
Before:
[streamed final starts] -> [preview message marked delivered] -> [follow-up chunk fails] -> [fallback suppressed] -> [user sees truncation]

After:
[streamed final starts] -> [follow-up chunks must succeed] -> [mark delivered] -> [result]
                                         \
                                          -> [follow-up failure] -> [treat as delivery failure / fallback path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw contributor environment
- Model/provider: N/A for unit coverage; real Telegram proof still pending
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram
- Relevant config (redacted): Telegram bot/channel configuration with partial streaming enabled

### Steps

1. Reproduce a streamed Telegram final that requires follow-up chunks after the preview message.
2. Simulate or trigger a thrown or false-return follow-up send.
3. Verify delivered-state is not marked successful and the fallback/repair path remains available.

### Expected

- Failed follow-up chunks do not leave the system thinking delivery succeeded, and preview-finalized truncated text can be repaired from transcript-backed final text.

### Actual

- Covered by unit tests in this branch; attached real-environment proof is still pending.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Ran targeted Vitest coverage for Telegram delivery tests locally and reviewed the source-level failure mode plus repair path.
- Edge cases checked: Follow-up chunk send returning false, follow-up chunk send throwing, and preview-finalized transcript repair path.
- What you did **not** verify: Real Telegram after-fix behavior proof in a live bot/chat environment.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Real Telegram proof is still missing, so source/test confidence may not match maintainer merge requirements.
- Mitigation: Attach redacted after-fix evidence from a real Telegram run and re-request ClawSweeper review.

target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.54s
```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:23:00
   Duration  3.17s
```

**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

Simulated v2026.5.28 upgrade from scratch state dir with shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`:

```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-proof-upgrade openclaw doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
```

Doctor output:
```
[state-migrations] Auto-migrated legacy state:
- Migrated 1 Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) entry → plugin state
- Archived Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) legacy source →
  command-deploy-cache.json.migrated
```

SQLite verification after migration:
```
sqlite3 ... "SELECT plugin_id, namespace, entry_key, value_json
  FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"
→ discord|slash-command-deploy-hashes|111122223333:default|
  {"version":1,"hashes":{"global:reconcile":"aaa...a",
  "guild:1471499665584296039":"bbb...b"}}
```

Legacy file status:
```
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json       # → No such file (migrated)
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json.migrated  # → archived copy exists
```

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.
- Existing live gateway proof (above) confirms `changed-only` reads fingerprints from this same plugin state namespace and skips Discord deploy REST when unchanged on subsequent restart.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-account live gateway restart post-upgrade (simulated config with single default account only)
- `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
- Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit

target.title: feat(agents): expose tools and their schemas via new after_tools_resolved hook [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Plugins have no way to discover which tools are available to an agent or inspect their input schemas. The plugin API exposes `registerTool()` but no `listTools()` or `getTools()`. Built-in tools (exec, read, write, web_fetch, etc.) are assembled dynamically per agent run and never exposed to plugin code.
- **Why it matters:** Plugins that need tool awareness — observability, auditing, policy enforcement, analytics — are forced to discover tools reactively on each `before_tool_call`, missing tools that are never called and lacking schema information.
- **What changed:** Added a new `after_tools_resolved` void hook that fires once per agent attempt after the full tool list is assembled (built-in + plugin + client-hosted, post-policy-filtering). The event includes tool name, label, description, and JSON Schema parameters. Parameters are passed by reference, consistent with how all other hooks in the codebase handle complex objects (e.g., `messages` in `llm_input`, `agent_end`, `before_prompt_build`).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No existing hooks or tool assembly logic modified. Hook is fire-and-forget (not awaited), so agent startup latency is unaffected. Not fired during compaction runs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
* https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/21330
* https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/discussions/26940

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New plugin hook `after_tools_resolved` available via `api.on("after_tools_resolved", handler)`
- Hook event contains `{ tools, provider, model }` where each tool has `{ name, label?, description?, parameters? }`
- Parameters are passed by reference (consistent with all other hooks — no defensive cloning)
- Hook context includes `agentId`, `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, `workspaceDir`
- Fire-and-forget: plugin handler errors are caught and logged, never block agent startup

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No` — read-only observability hook, no new capabilities granted
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` — void hook, cannot modify or block tool execution
- Data access scope changed? `No` — exposes tool metadata (names, schemas) that plugins could already observe via `before_tool_call`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu (remote VM)
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22
- Model/provider: Anthropic / claude-opus-4-6
- Integration/channel: WhatsApp
- Relevant config: Default agent with full tool profile

### Steps

1. Create a workspace plugin at `{workspaceDir}/.openclaw/extensions/test-tools-hook/`:

   `openclaw.plugin.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "id": "test-tools-hook",
     "configSchema": {
       "type": "object",
       "additionalProperties": false,
       "properties": {}
     }
   }
   ```

   `index.ts`:
   ```typescript
   export default function testToolsHook(api) {
     api.on("after_tools_resolved", async (event, ctx) => {
       const toolNames = event.tools.map((t) => t.name).join(", ");
       console.log(`[test-tools-hook] agent=${ctx.agentId} provider=${event.provider} model=${event.model}`);
       console.log(`[test-tools-hook] ${event.tools.length} tools resolved: ${toolNames}`);
       for (const tool of event.tools) {
         const params = tool.parameters?.properties
           ? Object.keys(tool.parameters.properties).join(", ")
           : "(no schema)";
         console.log(`  - ${tool.name}: ${params}`);
       }
     });
   }
   ```

2. Restart the gateway: `openclaw gateway --force --verbose`
3. Send a WhatsApp message to trigger an agent run

### Expected

- Gateway logs show `[test-tools-hook]` lines with agent ID, provider, model, full tool list, and per-tool parameter names
- Agent startup is not delayed by the hook

### Actual

- Hook fired with 24 tools resolved including all built-in, plugin, and skill tools
- Each tool listed with its full parameter schema (parameter names extracted from JSON Schema `properties`)
- Agent responded normally with no added latency

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

**Live gateway log output (WhatsApp inbound → hook fires → agent responds):**

```
04:55:20 [test-tools-hook] agent=main provider=anthropic model=claude-opus-4-6
04:55:20 [test-tools-hook] 24 tools resolved: read, edit, write, exec, process, browser, canvas, nodes, cron, message, tts, gateway, agents_list, sessions_list, sessions_history, sessions_send, sessions_spawn, subagents, session_status, web_search, web_fetch, image, memory_search, memory_get
04:55:20   - read: path, offset, limit, file_path
04:55:20   - edit: path, oldText, newText, file_path, old_string, new_string
04:55:20   - write: path, content, file_path
04:55:20   - exec: command, workdir, env, yieldMs, background, timeout, pty, elevated, host, security, ask, node
04:55:20   - process: action, sessionId, data, keys, hex, literal, text, bracketed, eof, offset, limit, timeout
04:55:20   - browser: action, target, node, profile, targetUrl, targetId, limit, maxChars, mode, snapshotFormat, refs, interactive, compact, depth, selector, frame, labels, fullPage, ref, element, type, level, paths, inputRef, timeoutMs, accept, promptText, request
04:55:20   - canvas: action, gatewayUrl, gatewayToken, timeoutMs, node, target, x, y, width, height, url, javaScript, outputFormat, maxWidth, quality, delayMs, jsonl, jsonlPath
04:55:20   - nodes: action, gatewayUrl, gatewayToken, timeoutMs, node, requestId, title, body, sound, priority, delivery, facing, maxWidth, quality, delayMs, deviceId, duration, durationMs, includeAudio, fps, screenIndex, outPath, maxAgeMs, locationTimeoutMs, desiredAccuracy, command, cwd, env, commandTimeoutMs, invokeTimeoutMs, needsScreenRecording, invokeCommand, invokeParamsJson
04:55:20   - cron: action, gatewayUrl, gatewayToken, timeoutMs, includeDisabled, job, jobId, id, patch, text, mode, runMode, contextMessages
04:55:20   - message: action, channel, target, targets, accountId, dryRun, message, effectId, effect, media, filename, buffer, contentType, mimeType, caption, path, filePath, replyTo, threadId, asVoice, silent, quoteText, bestEffort, gifPlayback, messageId, emoji, remove, targetAuthor, targetAuthorUuid, groupId, limit, before, after, around, fromMe, includeArchived, ...
04:55:20   - tts: text, channel
04:55:20   - gateway: action, delayMs, reason, gatewayUrl, gatewayToken, timeoutMs, raw, baseHash, sessionKey, note, restartDelayMs
04:55:20   - agents_list:
04:55:20   - sessions_list: kinds, limit, activeMinutes, messageLimit
04:55:20   - sessions_history: sessionKey, limit, includeTools
04:55:20   - sessions_send: sessionKey, label, agentId, message, timeoutSeconds
04:55:20   - sessions_spawn: task, label, agentId, model, thinking, runTimeoutSeconds, timeoutSeconds, cleanup
04:55:20   - subagents: action, target, message, recentMinutes
04:55:20   - session_status: sessionKey, model
04:55:20   - web_search: query, count, country, search_lang, ui_lang, freshness
04:55:20   - web_fetch: url, extractMode, maxChars
04:55:20   - image: prompt, image, images, model, maxBytesMb, maxImages
04:55:20   - memory_search: query, maxResults, minScore
04:55:20   - memory_get: path, from, lines
```

**New unit tests pass:**

- `buildAfterToolsResolvedToolMetadata`: parameter pass-through, undefined parameters handling
- `wired-hooks-after-tools-resolved.test.ts`: hook runner invocation, `hasHooks` registration

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Hook fires with correct tool metadata on live WhatsApp agent run (24 tools with full parameter schemas), agent startup and response unaffected
- Edge cases checked: Undefined parameters (client tools), empty tool names filtered out, empty label/description omitted, `agents_list` tool with no parameters shown correctly
- What you did **not** verify: Retry emission deduplication (consumer-side concern), compaction path (deliberately excluded)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — purely additive, no existing behavior changed
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit. No config flags needed — if no plugins register the hook, the `hasHooks()` guard skips all hook logic.
- Files/config to restore: N/A
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Unhandled promise rejections in gateway logs (mitigated by explicit `.catch()` on the fire-and-forget call)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook fires per attempt, not per user request (retries cause repeated emissions)
  - Mitigation: By design — each attempt is a fresh tool resolution. Consumers should dedupe if needed (e.g., hash-based caching). Documented in hook description.

- Hydrate likely or requested directory tools from the catalog, including missing tool calls, while preserving sequential execution contracts.
- Keep client tools directly visible in directory mode and document the new mode.

Fork attribution: based on JuanHuaXu/openclaw `codex/tool-search-directory-mode`, compared from `codex/fix-post-tool-continuation-guard`.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, trim entries, drop blank/whitespace-only entries, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Normalize Gateway `agent.run` `toolsAllow` before dispatching to the runner, matching the `sessions_spawn` normalization behavior.
- Keep CLI-backed runtime handling aligned with current `main`: runtime `toolsAllow` is forwarded to the CLI harness so the harness can fail closed when it cannot enforce the allowlist.

## Current upstream status
Latest `upstream/main` already includes broad subagent/runtime work and embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support. This PR remains scoped to the missing public/control-plane plumbing for `sessions_spawn.toolsAllow`, plus Gateway normalization for raw `agent.run` inputs.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the policy is applied to the spawned child embedded runtime so the child receives only allowed tool schemas. Gateway `agent.run` also normalizes direct `toolsAllow` input before runner dispatch.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `230b54baa3175fafeb7160922f510b489a6d1887`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, local Gateway on loopback token auth with `gateway.mode=local`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json` through Gateway (not `--local`), and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server that records every `/v1/responses` request tool schema.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local mock OpenAI Responses server from `/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/mock-openai-toolsallow.cjs`, started OpenClaw Gateway from the same checkout, then ran the parent agent through Gateway:

```text
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<loopback-proof-token> OPENAI_API_KEY=mock node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].' --timeout 90
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head Gateway proof summarizer. The parent initial request had `sessions_spawn`; the spawned child request had only `read`, with `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, and `sessions_yield` absent. The mock log had 3 requests total: parent initial tool call, parent follow-up after child result, and the restricted spawned child request.

```json
{
  "head": "230b54baa3175fafeb7160922f510b489a6d1887",
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "gatewayAgentRunId": "8843fa2b-8a41-49d6-aaa4-ae3487155aa5",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "parentTools": [
    "exec",
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "sessions_yield",
    "subagents"
  ],
  "restrictedChildTools": [
    "read"
  ],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "totalMockRequests": 3,
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs"
}
```

Additional current-head verification already run after resolving conflicts:

```text
node scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
prompt snapshot check passed

node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
oxlint shards passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --testNamePattern 'toolsAllow|timestamp context|canonical Anthropic' --reporter=dot
focused toolsAllow vitest shards passed
```

GitHub checks observed on current head: PR is mergeable/clean and the current `Real behavior proof` workflow check is passing.

Observed result after fix: A real Gateway-backed parent agent run called `sessions_spawn` with `toolsAllow:["read"]`, the child run completed with `CHILD_DONE`, and the actual spawned child model request only advertised `read`. Disallowed parent/session/runtime tools were not present in the spawned child request.

What was not tested: A paid external provider was not used. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server to avoid provider cost while exercising the real Gateway, real embedded runner, real `sessions_spawn` tool call, and real child model request construction.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`.
- Scenario locked in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported ACP runtime, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, is inherited by persisted child sessions, and is forwarded to CLI harnesses for fail-closed handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects `toolsAllow` clearly because ACP cannot enforce this embedded-runner allowlist.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. Unsupported ACP paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced. CLI-backed runs receive the runtime allowlist at the harness boundary so the harness can fail closed when enforcement is unavailable.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect all CLI harnesses to enforce runtime allowlists.
  - Mitigation: OpenClaw forwards the allowlist to the CLI harness boundary for fail-closed handling instead of silently dropping it.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: feat(embedded-runner): expose prep stage timings
target.body:
## Summary
- Expose embedded runner prep stage snapshots on `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult`.
- Include the same prep stage summary on the `agent_end` hook event for plugin observability.
- Add focused coverage for attempt result and hook event prep stage propagation.
- Document the new optional hook field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has May embedded-runner/subagent performance work and trace logging for startup stages. It still does not expose the attempt prep stage summary to `agent_end` hook observers. This PR is narrowed to that missing diagnostics surface so plugins can collect stage timing evidence without patching core locally.

## Motivation
OpenClaw records detailed embedded runner prep stages internally, but that timing data is not available to plugin hooks. When embedded runs feel slow, plugin authors and operators cannot tell whether time is going into tool bundling, system prompt construction, session resource loading, or stream setup without adding local instrumentation.

Exposing the existing stage snapshot gives downstream integrations a low-risk way to collect latency evidence before proposing behavior-changing optimizations.

## Implementation notes
- Reuses the existing `createEmbeddedRunStageTracker()` / `prepStages.snapshot()` path.
- Keeps all new fields optional.
- Uses public structural plugin hook types instead of exporting runner internals through the plugin API.
- Does not change tool bundling, system prompt construction, stream setup, model calls, or runner behavior.
- Documents `agent_end.prepStages` as optional diagnostic telemetry whose stage labels may grow over time.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Embedded runner prep stage timings are visible to a real non-bundled plugin's `agent_end` hook without changing the embedded run outcome.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `8d7ef409272853778bd234da21e777ac880216a6`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, temporary local plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths` with `hooks.allowConversationAccess=true`, typed lifecycle hook registration via `api.on("agent_end", ...)`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --json --session-key agent:default:prep-proof`, and the repo mock OpenAI-compatible server `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the mock OpenAI server with `SUCCESS_MARKER=OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK`, ran a local embedded agent turn against a temporary config, and had the temporary plugin write the real `agent_end` event to JSONL. The proof script asserted both the CLI result and plugin hook payload.

Fresh current-head verification:

```text
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "exposes embedded prep stages" --reporter=dot
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 58 skipped (59)
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head local OpenClaw CLI run and typed non-bundled plugin `agent_end` hook JSONL assertion.

```json
{
  "head": "8d7ef409272853778bd234da21e777ac880216a6",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK",
  "hookSuccess": true,
  "hookHasPrepStages": true,
  "hookTotalMs": 143,
  "hookStageNames": [
    "workspace-sandbox",
    "skills",
    "core-plugin-tools",
    "bootstrap-context",
    "bundle-tools",
    "system-prompt",
    "session-resource-loader",
    "agent-session",
    "stream-setup"
  ],
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78381-proof-current.Xjp9Fj"
}
```

Observed result after fix: A standalone embedded `openclaw agent --local` run completed successfully against the mock OpenAI server, returned `OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK`, and the temporary plugin's real typed `agent_end` handler observed `event.prepStages` with total setup time and expected prep stages.

What was not tested: A live paid external model/provider run was not run. The proof used OpenClaw's deterministic local OpenAI-compatible response server to avoid provider latency/cost while still exercising the real embedded runner, local CLI, plugin loader, and typed `agent_end` hook dispatch.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- Risk: plugin authors might treat stage names as permanent.
- Mitigation: docs describe `prepStages` as optional diagnostics and allow stage labels to grow over time.

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent and tool lifecycle boundaries
target.body:
## Why Agent execution lacked complete lifecycle boundaries around thinking, response generation, and tool execution, reducing traceability for observability and policy systems. This PR adds those boundaries and ensures tool lifecycle hooks carry `toolCallId` context when available. lobster-biscuit ## Split Context This PR was accidentally closed after a messed up rebase https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/12583 which was originally split from closed umbrella PR #9761: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/9761. ## Detailed Changes - Added internal agent lifecycle hook emissions: - `agent:thinking:start` / `agent:thinking:end` - `agent:response:start` / `agent:response:end` - `agent:tool:start` / `agent:tool:end` - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for tool execution lifecycle completion - Propagated `toolCallId` through before/after tool hook contexts where available - Extended client tool definition adapter to pass `toolCallId` and run after-tool hook flow - Added focused tests for agent lifecycle emissions and tool lifecycle behavior ## Related Links, Issues and Resolution - Closed source PR: #12583 AND #9761 - Related issues: - Closes #7724 - Closes #7597 - Closes #5513 - #7985 - #6095 - #6264 - #6885

Greptile Summary

This PR adds complete lifecycle boundaries for agent execution (thinking, response generation, and tool execution), improving observability and traceability for policy systems. The implementation propagates `toolCallId` through tool lifecycle hooks and ensures consistent hook firing across different execution paths. Key improvements: - Split lifecycle hook logic into dedicated `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module - Ensured `agent:thinking:start/end` fire for all agent runs - Added conditional `agent:response:start/end` hooks that only fire when response output is generated - Propagated `toolCallId` to `before_tool_call` and `after_tool_call` hooks - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for both success and error paths in tool execution - Comprehensive test coverage validates hook emissions in different scenarios

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge - well-structured refactoring with comprehensive test coverage - The changes are well-architected with proper error handling throughout. The new `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module cleanly separates concerns, all hook emission paths include error handling that logs warnings without failing execution, and the test coverage validates the core lifecycle hook behavior. The `toolCallId` propagation is handled consistently with proper fallbacks. - No files require special attention Last reviewed commit: d48824c

target.title: fix(media-understanding): resolve image model input from catalog, preserve explicit text-only (#92104)
target.body:
## Summary

Fix model resolution so that models in `models.providers` without explicit `input` correctly inherit `["text","image"]` from the bundled catalog, rather than defaulting to `["text"]`.

## Root Cause

`resolveProviderModelInput` defaults to `["text"]` when `input` is omitted from a `models.providers` entry. The inline provider match shadows the bundled static catalog where the same model has `["text","image"]`. This causes image/pdf tool calls to fail with "Unknown model" — models that are image-capable in the catalog lose that capability when referenced via `models.providers`.

**Fix**: In `resolveModelAsync`, adjacent to the existing `mediaInput` merge, also merge `input` from the bundled static catalog — but only when the inline config entry lacks an explicit `input` field. Uses the canonical `matchesProviderScopedModelId` for id matching. Models already resolved via the registry (bundled catalog or models.json) carry correct `input` and are untouched.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior

When a user configures `models.providers.<provider>.models` with an entry that omits `input` (e.g. `{ id: "claude-opus-4-8" }`), the bundled catalog's `input: ["text","image"]` is merged into the resolved model. When the user explicitly declares `input` (e.g. `{ id: "claude-opus-4-8", input: ["text"] }`), the explicit value is preserved — regardless of whether the id is bare or provider-scoped.

### environment

- OS: Linux x64
- Runtime: Node.js v24.13.1
- Package manager: pnpm v11.2.2
- Test runner: vitest v4.1.7
- openclaw: fix/issue-92104-v3 branch, commit on top of origin/main

### steps

1. Checkout `fix/issue-92104-v3` branch in openclaw workspace
2. Run the tsx repro script that imports real `resolveModelAsync` from production code:
   ```
   npx tsx scripts/repro-92104.mjs
   ```
3. Run unit tests: `pnpm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts`
4. Run media-understanding tests: `pnpm test -- --run src/media-understanding/image.test.ts src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts`

### observedResult

**Reproduction script output (real production code via tsx):**
```
========================================================================
Real behavior proof — PR #92176
fix(media-understanding): resolve image model input from catalog
========================================================================
node:      v24.13.1
workspace: /home/0668001085/workspace/openclaw
target:    anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 (catalog: ["text","image"])

── Scenario 1: models.providers entry OMITS input ──
   Config: { id: "claude-opus-4-8" }  (no `input` field)
   Expected: catalog supplies ["text","image"] → model is image-capable

  → resolved id:    claude-opus-4-8
  → resolved api:   anthropic-messages
  → resolved input: ["text","image"]
  ✅ PASS — catalog input merged, model now image-capable

── Scenario 2: explicit input: ["text"] ──
   Config: { id: "claude-opus-4-8", input: ["text"] }
   Expected: explicit ["text"] PRESERVED (not overwritten)

  → resolved input: ["text"]
  ✅ PASS — explicit ["text"] preserved

── Scenario 3: provider-scoped id, explicit input: ["text"] ──
   Config: { id: "anthropic/claude-opus-4-8", input: ["text"] }
   Expected: explicit ["text"] PRESERVED

  → resolved input: ["text"]
  ✅ PASS — explicit ["text"] preserved (scoped id)

========================================================================
All scenarios exercised against REAL resolveModelAsync in production code.
```

**Test suite:**
```
model.test.ts:
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  110 passed (110)    ← includes 3 new regression tests
   Duration  40.86s

image.test.ts + defaults.test.ts + runner.vision-skip.test.ts:
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  53 passed (53)
   Duration  9.72s

Total: 163 tests, zero regressions
```

**Production change (src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.ts):**
```typescript
// In resolveModelAsync, after mediaInput merge:
if (model && options?.allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback) {
    const staticModel = resolveStaticCatalogModel();
    // ... existing mediaInput merge ...
    
    // NEW: merge catalog input when user omitted explicit input (#92104)
    if (
      !model.input?.includes("image") &&
      staticModel?.input?.includes("image") &&
      !hasExplicitModelInput(cfg, normalizedRef.provider, normalizedRef.model)
    ) {
      model = { ...model, input: staticModel.input };
    }
}
```

Plus new `hasExplicitModelInput()` helper (~25 lines) that checks whether the user explicitly declared `input` on the matching `models.providers` entry, handling both bare and provider-scoped ids via `matchesProviderScopedModelId`.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts` — 110/110 (+3 new tests)
- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` — 26/26
- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts` — 16/16
- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts` — 8/8

New test cases in `model.test.ts`:
- `merges catalog image input when models.providers entry omits input`
- `preserves explicit input: ['text'] in models.providers`
- `preserves explicit input with provider-scoped model id`

---

target.title: fix(queue): kick followup drain on enqueue to prevent race-induced stall
target.body:
## Summary

When a new inbound message arrives during the terminal window of a previous run, the `enqueue-followup` path called `enqueueFollowupRun` with `restartIfIdle=false`. This relied on a non-atomic `isRunActive()` snapshot to decide whether to schedule a drain. If the snapshot returned `true` (stale — the run was still in its terminal/clearing window) but the run's completion drain had already fired against an empty queue, the newly enqueued item sat idle with no scheduled drain. The session went silent for ~5 minutes until the stuck-session watchdog intervened.

**Fix:** Change `restartIfIdle` from `false` to `true` in the `enqueue-followup` branch of `runReplyAgent` (`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts:1292`). This ensures `kickFollowupDrainIfIdle` runs inside `enqueueFollowupRun` when the queue is not already draining, eliminating the race window.

The existing re-check (`isRunActive()` + `scheduleFollowupDrain` on lines 1294-1298) remains as a belt-and-suspenders guard.

**Scope:** 1 line changed, 1 file. No new dependencies, no API changes.

## Linked Issue

- Closes #91909

## Root Cause

`enqueueFollowupRun(..., restartIfIdle=false)` defers drain scheduling to the caller. The caller checks `isRunActive()` — a snapshot that can be stale if the previous run is in its terminal window. When the snapshot says "a run is active" but that run's drain already completed against an empty queue, the new item sits in `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES[key]` with `draining=false` and no one to kick it.

Changing to `restartIfIdle=true` means `enqueueFollowupRun` itself calls `kickFollowupDrainIfIdle(key)` when `!queue.draining`, which is the correct invariant: if the queue has items and nobody is draining, start a drain.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Inbound dispatch race where a message enqueued during a previous run's terminal window never gets drained, causing the agent to go silent for ~5 minutes until the stuck-session watchdog fires.

**Real environment tested:** Linux 6.6.114 (WSL2), Node v24.16.0, OpenClaw repo at commit 301213a0 (upstream/main). Source-level analysis of `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/enqueue.ts`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/drain.ts`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Read the `enqueue-followup` branch in `agent-runner.ts` (line 1285-1305) — confirmed `restartIfIdle` is now `true`.
2. Read `enqueueFollowupRun` in `queue/enqueue.ts` — confirmed when `restartIfIdle=true` and `!queue.draining`, it calls `kickFollowupDrainIfIdle(key)`.
3. Read `kickFollowupDrainIfIdle` in `queue/drain.ts` — confirmed it retrieves the cached callback and calls `scheduleFollowupDrain`.
4. Read `scheduleFollowupDrain` in `queue/drain.ts` — confirmed it calls `beginQueueDrain` which sets `queue.draining = true` and starts the drain loop.

**Evidence after fix:** The diff is exactly 1 line:
```diff
-      false,
+      true,
```

The control flow after the fix: `enqueueFollowupRun` → `restartIfIdle=true` → `!queue.draining` → `kickFollowupDrainIfIdle(key)` → `scheduleFollowupDrain(key, cb)` → drain loop starts. This is the same path that works correctly for all other `enqueueFollowupRun` call sites (lines 1433, queue tests).

**Observed result after fix:** When a message arrives during a previous run's terminal window, `enqueueFollowupRun` now proactively kicks the drain if the queue is idle. The race between `isRunActive()` returning a stale value and the previous drain completing is eliminated because the drain is started from inside the enqueue, not from a post-enqueue snapshot.

**What was not tested:** Live end-to-end reproduction of the timing-sensitive race was not performed (requires multi-second coordination between run completion and inbound message arrival). The fix is validated by (a) source-level analysis showing the race path is eliminated, (b) the same `restartIfIdle=true` pattern is used by all other call sites without issues, and (c) the existing `queue.drain-restart.test.ts` test suite covers `kickFollowupDrainIfIdle` behavior.

**Proof limitations:** No live reproduction of the ~5min silent-agent scenario. The fix is a defensive parameter change that aligns this call site with the default behavior of `enqueueFollowupRun`.

target.title: Revamp command authorization candidates
target.body:
## Summary

- replace the ad hoc POSIX authorization path with a Tree-sitter-backed command candidate planner built on `explainShellCommand`
- route shell/argv allowlist checks and allow-always persistence through planned candidates, including static shell-wrapper payloads
- convert exec control-shell policy to a generic static command matcher over normalized argv/options/operands
- keep approval storage compatible while preserving safer fallback behavior for dynamic wrappers, path-scoped payloads, and pipe-to-shell cases
- keep the UI showing the actual command while honoring `allowedDecisions`

## Decisions and rationale

- POSIX shell authorization now comes from one planned command model: `planShellAuthorization()` uses the Tree-sitter-backed `explainShellCommand()` path, and the plan is carried into allowlist evaluation, allow-always persistence, and shell rewrite. The old POSIX splitter/render fallback is no longer an alternate source of candidates.
- Inline shell candidates are generated once during policy evaluation and then reused. The node-host safe-bin rewrite no longer re-runs `evaluateSystemRunAllowlist()` on the rendered command; it verifies that the plan still belongs to the original shell payload and renders from that plan.
- Exec control-shell policy now follows `Tree-sitter static extraction -> normalized command invocation -> hard-coded policy rule`.
- `/approve` handling is removed from exec control-shell policy. `/approve` is a control/chat action, not an executable policy concept, and exec should not carry a special deny list for old agent behavior. The old command-analysis carrier fixture block that used `/approve` has been deleted instead of renamed to another binary.
- Safe bins are treated as narrow, argument-profiled execution shortcuts, not blanket shell trust. Static, parsed safe-bin candidates may be rewritten to resolved trusted paths. Dynamic shells, command substitution, line continuations, path-scoped wrapper payloads, and pipe-to-shell cases remain approval-gated unless the whole relevant command graph is allowed.
- Static reads under `.ssh` / `~/.ssh` through basic read commands are now approval-required hard-coded control policy. Dynamic paths are not resolved by this matcher; they remain governed by the normal exec approval flow.
- Background `&` is preserved as shell topology. The planner models it as a background relationship and the renderer emits `&` when rebuilding commands, instead of failing closed or collapsing it into a sequence separator.
- `deny` still wins when deny-mode policy is active. The authorization planner can explain candidates, but runtime policy decides whether execution is allowed.

## Behavior changes

- Static POSIX shell wrappers like `sh -c 'git status'` can now be authorized by the inner `git` candidate when the payload is static and safely parsed.
- Dynamic wrappers like `sh -c '$CMD'`, command substitution, line continuations, and path-scoped wrapper payloads stay approval-gated instead of becoming reusable inner approvals.
- `curl URL | sh` / `curl URL | bash` behavior is preserved: both sides must be allowed for auto-run; allow-always persists `curl` only; persisted `curl` alone does not authorize the shell side on rerun.
- Mixed allowlist + safe-bin shell-wrapper payloads are rebuilt before execution from the existing authorization plan so the executed wrapper uses trusted resolved paths.
- Exec control-shell policy denies OpenClaw channel login commands and requires approval for security audit suppression mutation through the same normalized command matcher.
- Static `cat` / `less` / `more` / `head` / `tail` reads under `.ssh` or `~/.ssh` now require explicit approval.
- `ask=always` approval requests no longer offer an unusable Always Allow action in the Control UI.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: Candidate-based command authorization for POSIX shell and non-Windows argv execution, including shell-wrapper unwrapping, dynamic fallback, pipe-to-shell behavior, allow-always persistence, node-host execution, control-shell dangerous command policy, and UI approval decisions.

Real environment tested: Local macOS focused tests; local OpenClaw Control UI against the candidate worktree; Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-safe-bins.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts
git diff --check
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/command-analysis/risks.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/command-explainer/extract.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/command-explainer/extract.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Focused local proof passed 15 files / 465 tests. `git diff --check` passed. Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01ks1f6v0x35t56nymmxbaw4a1` / GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26135215597 completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0. Follow-up cleanup proof passed 2 files / 20 tests, then 6 files / 119 tests, then control-shell/preflight/carrier focused proof passed 4 files / 148 tests with 8 skipped. After removing the repo doc and deleting the old carrier fixture block, focused proof passed 4 files / 147 tests with 8 skipped. After the reduction pass, focused proof passed 7 files / 212 tests with 8 skipped, and `git diff --check` passed. After the test-rubbish audit cleanup, focused proof passed 5 files / 177 tests, `git diff --check` passed, and the changed-test diff is now +1804 / -944 / net +860.

Observed result after fix: Static shell wrappers can auto-run from trusted inner candidates; dynamic/path-scoped wrapper payloads still prompt; `curl | sh` requires both sides; allow-always saved `/usr/bin/git` for a live `sh -c 'git status --short'` run and the rerun produced no second approval request. Static `.ssh` reads through basic read commands now require approval.

What was not tested: Full release validation was not run. Windows behavior remains on the legacy fallback path. The local UI proof used `--no-channels`, so Slack/Discord listeners were not part of the manual proof.

## UI proof notes

- Started local OpenClaw from this worktree with `openclaw-local-test --repo ... --no-open --no-channels --ttl 2h`.
- The installed local-test helper was machine-locally adjusted to stop writing stale `tools.exec.mode = "auto"`; that was not a repo change.
- The Control UI displayed the actual command `sh -c 'git status --short'`, including Host, Agent, Session, CWD, Resolved, Security, and Ask fields.
- Clicking Always Allow resolved the prompt.
- A live chat/tool run then created a saved `/usr/bin/git` allow-always entry, rerunning the same prompt produced no second `exec.approval.requested` event within 30 seconds, and the temporary `/usr/bin/git` test entry was removed from `~/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json` afterward.
